differences.rustBehavior: Ctrl-C interrupts an active turn; the slash command reports that binding when the input loop is idle.
differences.reason: The single foreground terminal cannot read a slash command while the Provider turn owns the input loop.
differences.safety: The cancellation token still persists one interrupted terminal receipt.

differences.rustBehavior: Reports the environment or OS-keyring credential boundary without accepting plaintext into the session.
differences.reason: The Rust credential contract forbids plaintext configuration and headless keyring access.
differences.safety: No credential value is printed, persisted, or sent to the model.

differences.rustBehavior: Lists the pinned provider; switching requires a validated new process/session binding.
differences.reason: Changing the Provider inside a durable session would mix model identity and recovery evidence.
differences.safety: Existing sessions retain their exact Provider, model, and protocol binding.

differences.rustBehavior: Lists the configured local model binding only.
differences.reason: Remote model enumeration would add an unbounded network call to an otherwise local command.
differences.safety: No Provider request or credential read is needed to inspect the selected model.

differences.rustBehavior: Retries initialization when unavailable; after healthy startup it retries the latest terminal turn.
differences.reason: This preserves initialization recovery and makes the required durable turn retry reachable.
differences.safety: Unknown side effects are never replayed; recovered started tools remain indeterminate.
